mnemonics: Please Initiate, Validate, And Confirm Transactions
keyPoints: A transaction is created and digitally signed with a private key.
The transaction is broadcast to the peer-to-peer network and validated by nodes.
Validated transactions are grouped into a new block and added to the chain through the consensus mechanism.
commonMistakes: Believing a transaction is final as soon as it's broadcast; it requires confirmations.
Assuming transactions can be reversed or cancelled once sent to the network.
Confusing block time (when added to a block) with finality (when considered immutable).
